header.label: PTR Comparison Analytics
header.headline: Rural schools 67% PTR compliant — 79.0 L students in overcrowded schools
header.sub: 65% national PTR compliance in 2020-21. 79.0 L students (57%) in PTR-breaching schools. 7,778 schools with PTR above 40.

cards.label: Students in Non-Compliant Schools
cards.headline: 79.0 L
cards.note: 79.0 L students in schools exceeding PTR 30 — 57% of total enrollment

cards.label: Schools with PTR Above 40
cards.note: 7,778 schools exceed PTR 40 — the most severely overcrowded classrooms

cards.label: Teachers Required (PTR 30)
cards.fullLabel: Additional teachers needed to achieve PTR ≤ 30
cards.note: 98,607 teachers required to bring all schools to PTR 30 norm

cards.label: National PTR Compliance
cards.barLabel: 65% of schools within RTE PTR norm
cards.note: 65% of schools meet the PTR ≤ 30 norm — 14,208 schools still non-compliant

2020-21.chartLocPtrSource: PTR compliance rate (schools with PTR ≤ 30) by school location — rural schools consistently outperform urban schools on PTR compliance due to smaller enrollment sizes.
2020-21.chartLocNote: Rural schools show higher PTR compliance than urban schools. Urban concentration of large schools drives higher PTR.
2020-21.chartCatPtrSource: PTR compliance rate by school category — Secondary schools have the lowest compliance as they serve larger, more concentrated student populations.
2020-21.chartCatNote: Secondary schools face the greatest PTR challenge, while Foundational schools benefit from smaller class sizes.

insight1.label: National Compliance Rate
insight1.note: 26,230 schools meet PTR ≤ 30 norm out of 40,438 total schools in 2020-21

insight2.label: Teachers Required
insight2.note: Additional teachers needed to bring all 14,208 non-compliant schools to PTR 30 norm
